knowns: Day-1 abandonment rate is 74–77%, establishing a quantified baseline for retention improvement measurement
No persistent AI memory layer exists — coaching context resets between sessions
AI infrastructure budget is hard-capped at $200/month with no current cost-per-inference tracking
Tech stack is React + FastAPI + PostgreSQL + Supabase with likely OpenAI API integration
Founder is a solo AI/ML engineer — no design, growth, or secondary engineering capacity
Target demographic is health-conscious adults 18–45 who have failed with generic fitness apps
Core competitors include Fitbod, Noom, Future, Freeletics, Caliber, and WHOOP
No behavioral economics layer is implemented — zero commitment devices, loss aversion triggers, or variable reward mechanics
Cold-start personalization problem is acknowledged — first-session AI output is effectively generic
No product instrumentation or funnel analytics currently exist
Product is bootstrapped with limited runway — rapid iteration cycles are mandatory
Primary success threshold is Day-7 retention above 60% and first-session completion above 75%
critical_unknowns: Exact drop-off point(s) within the Day-1 session — is abandonment occurring at signup, onboarding intake, first workout generation, or mid-workout?
Whether abandonment is driven by onboarding friction, perceived value delay, motivational misalignment, or technical failure
Current onboarding flow length and number of input fields required before first workout is shown
Qualitative user exit reasons — no user interviews, churn surveys, or session recordings are mentioned
What the current first-session experience actually looks like from a user perspective — no UX audit data exists
Whether any users have completed Day-7 and what behavioral patterns differentiate retained vs. churned users
Actual current AI inference cost-per-session and per-user — no baseline cost data documented
Which LLM provider and model tier is currently in use — no confirmed OpenAI vs. alternative, no model tier specified
Whether Supabase schema currently supports any user history or performance data storage suitable for memory layer migration
What competitor retention rates actually look like at Day-7 — Fitbod, Future, Freeletics benchmarks are unconfirmed
Which behavioral retention mechanics have empirical validation specifically in fitness app contexts vs. general consumer apps
Whether the current user base has generated any longitudinal data usable as population-level priors for cold-start logic
User psychological profile distribution — what percentage of current/prospective users are intrinsically vs. extrinsically motivated
App Store and Play Store review sentiment for top competitors — what are users explicitly praising or condemning in comparable products
Whether the current exercise catalog has sufficient breadth to support equipment-constrained and beginner user paths
What minimum viable intake signal set is sufficient to generate a differentiated first workout without excessive onboarding friction
Whether any A/B testing infrastructure or feature flag system exists, even in rudimentary form
Regulatory or liability exposure from AI-generated workout programming without certified exercise science guardrails

research_tracks.question: What are the exact behavioral patterns, stated frustrations, and unmet expectations of users who abandon fitness apps within the first 7 days — specifically what triggers the decision to quit?

What onboarding flows, first-session structures, and cold-start personalization mechanisms do Fitbod, Future, Freeletics, and Caliber use — and where do they deliver or fail to deliver perceived value within the first session?

What is the empirical evidence for specific behavioral economics mechanisms — commitment devices, variable reward schedules, loss aversion triggers, implementation intentions — in improving 7-day and 30-day retention specifically in mobile fitness or health behavior change apps?

What are the proven architectural patterns for lightweight persistent memory in LLM-powered applications — including vector store approaches, structured key-value memory, and hybrid retrieval — and what are their latency, cost, and complexity tradeoffs at sub-$200/month AI budget constraints?

What do App Store and Play Store reviews for Fitbod, Future, Noom, Freeletics, and Caliber reveal about the specific language users use to describe AI personalization value, onboarding frustration, and reasons for cancellation or return?

What is the minimum viable onboarding input set — across fitness goal, experience level, equipment, schedule, and psychological motivation type — that exercise science and personalization research indicates is sufficient to generate a meaningfully differentiated initial training recommendation?

What are the current OpenAI and Anthropic model tier pricing structures, prompt caching capabilities, and cost-per-1K-token rates for Haiku, GPT-4o-mini, and equivalent lightweight tiers — and what routing architectures achieve sub-$0.50 per active user monthly at assumed session frequency?

Are there documented cases of fitness apps or health behavior platforms that reduced Day-1 to Day-7 abandonment by more than 20 percentage points — and what specific product, onboarding, or retention interventions were causally attributed to those improvements?

data_layers_needed: Session-level event log with timestamped drop-off events across the current onboarding funnel — screen-by-screen abandonment rates
First-session completion rate broken down by completion stage — intake finished, workout generated, workout started, workout completed
Day-1, Day-3, Day-7, and Day-30 cohort retention curves from existing user base, however small
Current AI inference cost log — per-request token counts, model used, and associated cost over trailing 30 days
Supabase schema documentation — tables, relationships, and existing user history storage to assess memory layer migration complexity
App Store and Play Store review corpus for TransformFit (if live) and top 5 competitors — minimum 500 reviews per competitor for sentiment pattern extraction
User intake response distribution — what fitness goals, experience levels, and equipment configurations current users are self-reporting
Competitor onboarding screenshot and flow documentation — minimum Fitbod, Future, Freeletics captured within current quarter
Exercise catalog inventory — total exercise count, equipment tags, muscle group coverage, and difficulty tier distribution
Any existing user feedback, support tickets, or exit survey responses — even anecdotal qualitative signals from Michael directly
temporary_assumptions: Assuming OpenAI GPT-4o-mini or Anthropic Claude Haiku is the primary inference model until confirmed — cost modeling will use published rates for these tiers

Assuming the majority of Day-1 abandonment occurs between workout generation and first workout completion, not at signup — this will be invalidated or confirmed once session event logging is instrumented
Assuming Supabase PostgreSQL schema can accommodate a structured memory layer without full migration — vector store will be treated as additive, not replacement, pending schema review
Assuming a 5–7 question intake flow is the current onboarding length — cold-start personalization strategy will be scoped around this until UX audit confirms actual field count
Assuming no feature flag or A/B testing infrastructure currently exists — all onboarding variant testing will require sequential rather than parallel rollout
Assuming competitor Day-7 retention rates are in the 30–45% range based on industry benchmarks for fitness apps — this will be revised if competitor-specific data surfaces

Assuming the current user base is under 500 active users — population-level prior construction will rely on public exercise science datasets rather than internal behavioral data until user volume grows
Assuming Michael has direct access to raw Supabase logs and can instrument basic event tracking within one sprint cycle without third-party analytics tooling
Assuming behavioral retention mechanics have not been tested in any form — if any prior experiment data exists, it must be surfaced before behavioral system design begins
Assuming exercise science liability exposure is manageable at current user scale without formal certification — this assumption requires legal validation before public launch at scale
